function.name: browser_back
function.description: Navigate back to the previous page in browser history. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_click
function.description: Click on an element identified by its ref ID from the snapshot (e.g., '@e5'). The ref IDs are shown in square brackets in the snapshot output. Requires browser_navigate and browser_snapshot to be called first.

ref.description: The element reference from the snapshot (e.g., '@e5', '@e12')

function.name: browser_console
function.description: Get browser console output and JavaScript errors from the current page. Returns console.log/warn/error/info messages and uncaught JS exceptions. Use this to detect silent JavaScript errors, failed API calls, and application warnings. Requires browser_navigate to be called first. When 'expression' is provided, evaluates JavaScript in the page context and returns the result — use this for DOM inspection, reading page state, or extracting data programmatically.

clear.description: If true, clear the message buffers after reading

expression.description: JavaScript expression to evaluate in the page context. Runs in the browser like DevTools console — full access to DOM, window, document. Return values are serialized to JSON. Example: 'document.title' or 'document.querySelectorAll("a").length'

function.name: browser_get_images
function.description: Get a list of all images on the current page with their URLs and alt text. Useful for finding images to analyze with the vision tool. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_navigate
function.description: Navigate to a URL in the browser. Initializes the session and loads the page. Must be called before other browser tools. For simple information retrieval, prefer web_search or web_extract (faster, cheaper). For plain-text endpoints — URLs ending in .md, .txt, .json, .yaml, .yml, .csv, .xml, raw.githubusercontent.com, or any documented API endpoint — prefer curl via the terminal tool or web_extract; the browser stack is overkill and much slower for these. Use browser tools when you need to interact with a page (click, fill forms, dynamic content). Returns a compact page snapshot with interactive elements and ref IDs — no need to call browser_snapshot separately after navigating.

function.name: browser_press
function.description: Press a keyboard key. Useful for submitting forms (Enter), navigating (Tab), or keyboard shortcuts. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_scroll
function.description: Scroll the page in a direction. Use this to reveal more content that may be below or above the current viewport. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

function.name: browser_snapshot
function.description: Get a text-based snapshot of the current page's accessibility tree. Returns interactive elements with ref IDs (like @e1, @e2) for browser_click and browser_type. full=false (default): compact view with interactive elements. full=true: complete page content. Snapshots over 8000 chars are truncated or LLM-summarized. Requires browser_navigate first. Note: browser_navigate already returns a compact snapshot — use this to refresh after interactions that change the page, or with full=true for complete content.

full.description: If true, returns complete page content. If false (default), returns compact view with interactive elements only.

function.name: browser_type
function.description: Type text into an input field identified by its ref ID. Clears the field first, then types the new text. Requires browser_navigate and browser_snapshot to be called first.

ref.description: The element reference from the snapshot (e.g., '@e3')

text.description: The text to type into the field

function.name: browser_vision
function.description: Take a screenshot of the current page and analyze it with vision AI. Use this when you need to visually understand what's on the page - especially useful for CAPTCHAs, visual verification challenges, complex layouts, or when the text snapshot doesn't capture important visual information. Returns both the AI analysis and a screenshot_path that you can share with the user by including MEDIA:<screenshot_path> in your response. Requires browser_navigate to be called first.

question.description: What you want to know about the page visually. Be specific about what you're looking for.

annotate.description: If true, overlay numbered [N] labels on interactive elements. Each [N] maps to ref @eN for subsequent browser commands. Useful for QA and spatial reasoning about page layout.

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

function.name: web_extract
function.description: Extract content from web page URLs. Returns page content in markdown format. Also works with PDF URLs (arxiv papers, documents, etc.) — pass the PDF link directly and it converts to markdown text. Pages under 5000 chars return full markdown; larger pages are LLM-summarized and capped at ~5000 chars per page. Pages over 2M chars are refused. If a URL fails or times out, use the browser tool to access it instead.

urls.description: List of URLs to extract content from (max 5 URLs per call)

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web for information. Returns up to 5 results by default with titles, URLs, and descriptions. The query is passed through to the configured backend, so operators such as site:domain, filetype:pdf, intitle:word, -term, and "exact phrase" may work when the backend supports them.

query.description: The search query to look up on the web. You may include backend-supported operators such as site:example.com, filetype:pdf, intitle:word, -term, or "exact phrase".

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 5.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits. Auto-runs syntax checks on .py/.json/.yaml/.toml and other linted languages; only NEW errors introduced by this write are surfaced (pre-existing errors are filtered out).

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

messages.content:
Do a comprehensive audit of the elviajero-comercio codebase. I need to understand:
1. What's the current state of every page and feature
2. What bugs/breakages exist in the live site
3. What's been done already vs what's still pending

Go through all the existing docs in /root/elviajero/docs/ — read IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, improvement-analysis.md, refactoring-analysis.md, test-plan-[REDACTED_PHONE].md, and STATUS.md to understand what was planned vs completed.

Then check the live site at https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com and test these flows:
- Homepage loads and looks good
- Search (type "carpa" in search bar, hit enter)
- Navigation (click Tienda, Nosotros, FAQ, Promociones, Blog, Contacto)
- Product page (click any product)
- Add to cart flow
- FAQ accordion (click questions)
- Dark mode toggle
- Currency toggle (Gs/USD)
- Language toggle (if exists)
- Mobile responsive (check if mobile menu works)
- Newsletter signup
- Login/register flow
- Admin panel access (/admin)
- Mi cuenta pages

Report BOTH what works and what doesn't work. For bugs, be specific about what happens vs what should happen.
I'll start by reading all the docs, then test the live site thoroughly.

{"content": "     1|# El Viajero — Refactoring Targets & Abstractions
     2|
     3|Date: [REDACTED_PHONE]
     4|Repo: /root/elviajero
     5|
     6|---
     7|
     8|## P0 — HIGH IMPACT REFACTORS
     9|
    10|### 1. Payment Gateway Factory
    11|**Files:** `app/api/checkout/bancard/route.ts` (33L) + `pagopar/route.ts` (34L) + `paypal/route.ts` (43L) + `stripe/route.ts` (40L)
    12|**Pattern:** 4 routes with ~90% identical boilerplate (validate cart, create order, return URL)
    13|**Proposal:** `lib/payment/factory.ts` — one generic handler that receives gateway name, delegates to gateway-specific adapter
    14|```typescript
    15|// Instead of 4 route files:
    16|POST → /api/checkout/[gateway]  // bancard | pagopar | paypal | stripe
    17|```
    18|**Effort:** 2h
    19|**Benefit:** Adding new gateway = write adapter only (20 lines)
    20|
    21|### 2. DB CRUD Factory
    22|**Files:** `app/api/db/orders/route.ts` (37L) + `products/route.ts` (27L) + `promos/route.ts` (38L) + `reviews/route.ts` (33L) + `users/route.ts` (32L)
    23|**Pattern:** 5 routes, each has GET (list) + POST (create) with almost identical Supabase queries
    24|**Proposal:** `lib/db/crud-factory.ts`
    25|```typescript
    26|createCrudRoutes({ table: "products", adminOnly: true })
    27|// Returns { GET, POST } handlers
    28|```
    29|**Effort:** 1h
    30|**Benefit:** CRUD for new entities = 1 line
    31|
    32|### 3. Supabase Client Consolidation
    33|**Files:** `lib/supabase/client.ts` + `browser.ts` + `server.ts` + `middleware.ts` + `admin.ts` = 5 files
    34|**Issue:** 5 different ways to create Supabase clients, some redundant (`client.ts` re-exports `browser.ts`)
    35|**Proposal:** Consolidate to 3:
    36|- `lib/supabase/browser.ts` — browser client
    37|- `lib/supabase/server.ts` — server component client  
    38|- `lib/supabase/admin.ts` — admin (service_role, for API routes)
    39|- Remove: `client.ts`, simplify `middleware.ts` to reuse `server.ts`
    40|**Effort:** 30min
    41|**Benefit:** Less confusion, single source of truth
    42|
    43|---
    44|
    45|## P1 — MEDIUM IMPACT REFACTORS
    46|
    47|### 4. localStorage Key Constants
    48|**Files:** 6 files with 10+ localStorage key strings
    49|**Issue:** `viajero_currency`, `viajero_lang`, `viajero_favs`, `viajero_favs_${id}`, `viajero_cart_activity`, `viajero_cart_reminder_sent`, `viajero_promos`
    50|**Proposal:** `lib/storage-keys.ts`
    51|```typescript
    52|export const STORAGE_KEYS = {
    53|  CURRENCY: "viajero_currency",
    54|  LANG: "viajero_lang",
    55|  FAVORITES: (userId?: string) => userId ? `viajero_favs_${userId}` : "viajero_favs",
    56|  CART_ACTIVITY: "viajero_cart_activity",
    57|  CART_REMINDER: "viajero_cart_reminder_sent",
    58|  PROMOS: "viajero_promos",
    59|} as const
    60|```
    61|**Effort:** 15min
    62|
    63|### 5. Abandoned Cart / Cart Recovery Module
    64|**Files:** `lib/abandoned-cart.ts`
    65|**Issue:** Scattered across localStorage reads/writes, could be a clean service
    66|**Also:** Related config lives in `content/es.json` (abandonedCart section)
    67|**Proposal:** Consolidate into single service with config injection
    68|
    69|### 6. Auth Context De-dup
    70|**File:** `lib/auth-context.tsx` (200+ lines)
    71|**Issue:** Giant context with login, register, OAuth, profile, addresses, favorites, orders ALL in one file
    72|**Proposal:** Split into:
    73|- `lib/auth/auth-context.tsx` — core auth (login, register, logout, session)
    74|- `lib/auth/profile-hooks.ts` — profile, addresses
    75|- `lib/auth/orders-hooks.ts` — orders
    76|- `lib/auth/favorites.ts` — favorites (localStorage)
    77|
    78|---
    79|
    80|## P2 — LOW IMPACT / CODE QUALITY
    81|
    82|### 7. Header Component Abstract Sections
    83|**File:** `components/header.tsx` (357 lines)
    84|**Issue:** Largest component. Mixes nav, search, currency, lang, cart, auth, dark mode
    85|**Proposal:** Extract into smaller sub-components:
    86|- `components/header/nav.tsx` — navigation links
    87|- `components/header/actions.tsx` — search, currency, lang, dark mode
    88|- `components/header/cart-badge.tsx` — cart icon + count
    89|- `components/header/auth-menu.tsx` — login / user menu
    90|
    91|### 8. Admin Layout Split
    92|**File:** `components/admin/admin-layout.tsx` (143 lines)
    93|**Issue:** Contains auth check, sidebar, header, content area — could split into sidebar + header
    94|**Proposal:**
    95|- `components/admin/sidebar.tsx`
    96|- `components/admin/admin-header.tsx`
    97|
    98|### 9. Cart Context Simplify
    99|**File:** `lib/cart-context.tsx` (117 lines)
   100|**Issue:** Interface CartItem, Context, Provider, add/remove/update/clear all in one file
   101|**Proposal:** Extract CartItem type to separate file, keep context focused
   102|
   103|### 10. Magic Numbers
   104|**File:** `lib/currency.tsx`
   105|**Issue:** `RATE_PYG_PER_USD = 7400` hardcoded — should come from config or API
   106|**Proposal:** Move to `content/es.json` (paymentGateway section has rate field already) or fetch from external API
   107|
   108|---
   109|
   110|## ARCHITECTURE DIAGRAM (current vs proposed)
   111|
   112|### Current:
   113|```
   114|app/api/
   115|├── checkout/
   116|│   ├── bancard/route.ts    ← 4 nearly identical files
   117|│   ├── pagopar/route.ts
   118|│   ├── paypal/route.ts
   119|│   └── stripe/route.ts
   120|├── db/
   121|│   ├── orders/route.ts     ← 5 nearly identical CRUD files
   122|│   ├── products/route.ts
   123|│   ├── promos/route.ts
   124|│   ├── reviews/route.ts
   125|│   └── users/route.ts
   126|├── auth/route.ts
   127|├── orders/route.ts
   128|└── ...
   129|
   130|lib/
   131|├── cart-context.tsx         ← 117 lines, 5 concerns
   132|├── auth-context.tsx         ← 200+ lines, 6 concerns
   133|├── supabase/
   134|│   ├── client.ts             ← redundant (re-exports browser.ts)
   135|│   ├── browser.ts
   136|│   ├── server.ts
   137|│   ├── middleware.ts
   138|│   └── admin.ts
   139|│   
   140|components/
   141|├── header.tsx                ← 357 lines, 6 sub-components
   142|├── admin/admin-layout.tsx    ← 143 lines, 3 sections
   143|```
   144|
   145|### Proposed:
   146|```
   147|app/api/
   148|├── checkout/[gateway]/route.ts  ← 1 file, delegates to adapters
   149|├── db/
   150|│   ├── [[resource]]/route.ts   ← 1 file, factory-generated
   151|│   └── ...
   152|
   153|lib/
   154|├── supabase/
   155|│   ├── browser.ts
   156|│   ├── server.ts
   157|│   └── admin.ts                ← client.ts + middleware.ts removed
   158|├── payment/
   159|│   ├── factory.ts              ← handles routing + validation
   160|│   ├── bancard.ts              ← gateway adapter (20 lines)
   161|│   ├── pagopar.ts
   162|│   ├── paypal.ts
   163|│   └── stripe.ts
   164|├── db/
   165|│   └── crud-factory.ts         ← generates GET/POST from table name
   166|├── auth/
   167|│   ├── auth-context.tsx
   168|│   ├── profile-hooks.ts
   169|│   ├── orders-hooks.ts  
   170|│   └── favorites.ts
   171|├── storage-keys.ts             ← single source for all localStorage keys
   172|├── cart-context.tsx             ← same size but cleaner
   173|
   174|components/
   175|├── header/
   176|│   ├── index.tsx
   177|│   ├── nav.tsx
   178|│   ├── actions.tsx
   179|│   ├── cart-badge.tsx
   180|│   └── auth-menu.tsx
   181|├── admin/
   182|│   ├── admin-layout.tsx
   183|│   ├── sidebar.tsx
   184|│   └── admin-header.tsx
   185|```
   186|
   187|## QUICK WINS (15 min each)
   188|1. Create `lib/storage-keys.ts` — eliminates magic string risk
   189|2. Remove `lib/supabase/client.ts` — it's a redundant re-export
   190|3. Consolidate `lib/currency.tsx` rate into content config
   191|4. Extract `lib/cart-context.tsx` CartItem type to `lib/types.ts`
   192|
   193|## MEDIUM WINS (1-2h each)
   194|5. Payment gateway factory (2h) — biggest RoI
   195|6. DB CRUD factory (1h) — cleanest abstraction
   196|7. Auth context split (1h) — maintainability
   197|
   198|## BIG WINS (3-4h each)
   199|8. Payment gateway factory + test suite
   200|9. Full architecture reorg (header split, admin split, supabase consolidation)
   201|10. Shared package extraction to @elviajero/common (if any other site would benefit)
   202|", "total_lines": 201, "file_size": 7444, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

    25|

    30|- Payment gateway integration (stripe/pagopar/bancard — factory exists but untested)

Here's the comprehensive audit report:

---

## COMPREHENSIVE AUDIT: elviajero-comercio

### WHAT I DID
1. Read all 5 docs in `/root/elviajero/docs/` (IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md, improvement-analysis.md, refactoring-analysis.md, test-plan-[REDACTED_PHONE].md, STATUS.md)
2. Tested all requested flows on https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com

---

### CURRENT STATE FROM DOCS

**Planned vs Completed (per STATUS.md + test-plan):**
- ✅ Supabase Migration DONE — 34 products seeded, auth working, admin panel with real auth
- ✅ Docker deployed (2 replicas) on el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
- ⬜ Payment gateways (Bancard, PagoPar, PayPal, Stripe) — factory exists but UNTESTED
- ⬜ Google OAuth — needs Client ID/Secret in Supabase Dashboard
- ⬜ Facebook OAuth — same
- ⬜ GA4 — no env var set
- ⬜ Stats showing 0 — admin dashboard queries wrong table (`products` instead of `ej_products`)
- ⬜ Password reset flow — still uses old SQLite approach
- ⬜ Product images — still SVG placeholders, no real photos
- ⬜ Cart system — LOCALSTORAGE ONLY, no persistence across sessions

**Known Issues from test-plan-[REDACTED_PHONE]:**
- 3 dirty files existed (`.orig`, `.rej`, `.part`) — need to check if cleaned
- `metadataBase` URL points to `el-viajero` instead of `viajero.paragu-ai.com`
- Build fails for Docker (`standalone` output path issue)

---

### LIVE SITE TEST RESULTS

**✅ WORKING:**
1. **Homepage** — Loads successfully, no console errors, all sections visible (hero carousel, Nuevo en Stock, Productos Destacados, location map, categories, testimonials, newsletter, footer)
2. **Search** — Overlay opens, typing "carpa" shows autocomplete result for "Carpa 4 Personas"
3. **Navigation pages** — ALL load successfully: /tienda, /nosotros, /faq, /promociones, /blog, /contacto, /login, /register
4. **Tienda page** — Full product grid (34 products, 7 categories), filters (category, brand, price range, stock status), sort options, "Agregar a favoritos" hearts visible
5. **FAQ accordion** — Clicking questions toggles answers correctly (tested first question)
6. **Dark mode toggle** — Works, persists via class on `<html>`
7. **Language toggle** — Sets localStorage `viajero_lang` to `es`/`en`/`gn`
8. **Blog page** — Lists 6 blog posts with categories (Camping, Pesca, Aventura, Mantenimiento)
9. **Promociones** — Lists 3 promos (Kit Camping Completo, Combo Pesca Inicial, Envio Gratis)
10. **Contacto** — Contact form with name/phone/message fields
11. **Admin panel** — Redirects to login (correct auth gate)
12. **Cart badge** — Visible in header as a button
13. **Currency toggle button** — Present and clickable (button "Cambiar moneda")
14. **Google Maps iframe** — Embedded on homepage
15. **Cookie consent banner** — Appears, clickable "Aceptar" button

**❌ BROKEN / NOT WORKING:**

1. **BROKEN: Navigation links lead to homepage instead of target pages**
   - **What happens:** Clicking "Tienda" in the nav bar redirects back to `/` instead of `/tienda`
   - **What should happen:** Should navigate to `/tienda` with the full product grid
   - **Impact:** HIGH — users can't navigate via navbar; they must type URLs manually

2. **BROKEN: No product detail pages**
   - **What happens:** Clicking any product name/link on the tienda page does nothing — stays on same page, no modal, no navigation
   - **What should happen:** Should open either a product detail page (e.g., `/producto/bolsa-de-dormir-0c`) or a quick-view modal
   - **Impact:** HIGH — customers can't see full product details, specs, or larger images

3. **BROKEN: "Agregar al carrito" button does nothing**
   - **What happens:** Clicking the "Agregar" button on any product produces no visible effect. localStorage (`viajero-cart`) remains empty
   - **What should happen:** Item should be added to cart, cart badge should increment, cart sidebar should appear
   - **Impact:** CRITICAL — cart system is completely non-functional

4. **BROKEN: Cart sidebar doesn't appear**
   - **What happens:** Clicking the cart button in the header does nothing
   - **What should happen:** Should open a slide-out sidebar showing cart contents and totals
   - **Impact:** CRITICAL — no way to view cart, proceed to checkout

5. **BROKEN: Language toggle doesn't translate content**
   - **What happens:** Toggling to "EN" stores the preference in localStorage but all page content remains in Spanish
   - **What should happen:** Page text should switch to English (or at least Spanish if EN content is incomplete)
   - **Impact:** MEDIUM — feature exists but is cosmetic only

6. **BROKEN: No WhatsApp real number configured**
   - **What happens:** WhatsApp buttons exist but phone number shown in snapshots is template placeholder
   - **What should happen:** Should use the real WhatsApp Business number from the business owner
   - **Impact:** HIGH — WhatsApp ordering flow doesn't work

7. **BROKEN: Products use SVG placeholder images**
   - **What happens:** All product cards show SVG icons, not real product photos
   - **What should happen:** Real product images should display (from Unsplash or uploaded)
   - **Impact:** MEDIUM-HIGH — kills trust, customers can't see the actual product

8. **MISSING: No priceBefore/sale pricing shown**
   - **What happens:** No products show a strikethrough old price or "OFERTA" badge
   - **What should happen:** Products with `priceBefore` field should show old price crossed out + sale badge
   - **Impact:** LOW-MEDIUM — only 1 product (Toldo Sombra) has "OFERTA" text

9. **MISSING: No product detail page route**
   - **What happens:** `/producto/[slug]` route doesn't exist
   - **What should happen:** Each product should have a dedicated detail page
   - **Impact:** MEDIUM — users on homepage can't click through to learn more about a product

10. **MISSING: Google/Facebook OAuth buttons present but non-functional**
    - **What happens:** Buttons show "Continuar con Google/Facebook" but no OAuth credentials are configured in Supabase
    - **What should happen:** OAuth should work with proper client IDs
    - **Impact:** MEDIUM — users see the option but it fails

11. **MISSING: Payment gateways not connected**
    - **What happens:** No checkout flow tested — cart doesn't work, no payment buttons active
    - **What should happen:** At least one payment gateway should be operational
    - **Impact:** CRITICAL — no revenue possible

12. **MISSING: Admin dashboard shows 0s**
    - **What happens:** According to STATUS.md, stats read from `products` table instead of `ej_products`
    - **What should happen:** Admin should show real stats from seeded data
    - **Impact:** MEDIUM — admin can't monitor business

---

### SUMMARY OF COMPLETED VS PENDING

**Already Done:**
- Next.js 15 app running on Docker (2 replicas)
- Supabase migration complete (auth, tables, RLS policies)
- Auth flow (email/password login/register)
- Admin panel with role-based access
- Full UI with Tailwind 4, dark mode, currency/lang toggles
- 34 products seeded in Supabase
- Blog with 6 posts
- FAQ accordion
- Contact form
- Newsletter form
- Category/tag filtering on tienda
- Mobile hamburger menu (present but untested for click behavior)

**Still Pending / Blocked:**
- **Cart system completely broken** — add to cart and cart sidebar don't fire
- **No product detail pages** — critical UX gap
- **Nav links scroll instead of navigate** — broken navigation
- **No payment gateway integration** — no checkout flow
- **No real product images** — SVG placeholders only
- **No real WhatsApp number** configured
- **Language toggle cosmetic only** — no actual translation
- **OAuth not configured** (Google/Facebook)
- **Password reset flow** — still on old SQLite
- **Admin dashboard** — queries wrong table
- **GA4 / analytics** — placeholder IDs
- **Dirty files** — need to verify if `.orig`, `.rej`, `.part` were cleaned
- **metadataBase** — wrong domain (`el-viajero` vs `viajero`)
- **Real business data** — contact info, hours, WhatsApp number need owner input
